 {"id":"beads_rust-fmxd","title":"E2E tests: defer/undefer commands","description":"# E2E Tests for \`defer\` and \`undefer\` Commands

## Overview
The defer/undefer commands manage issue scheduling by setting/clearing \`status=deferred\` and \`defer_until\` timestamp.

## Commands to Test
- \`br defer <id>\` - Defer issue indefinitely
- \`br defer <id> --until <time>\` - Defer until specific time
- \`br defer <id1> <id2>\` - Defer multiple issues
- \`br undefer <id>\` - Undefer (set status=open)
- \`br undefer <id1> <id2>\` - Undefer multiple

## Test Cases

### Defer Basic (5 tests)
1. **defer_sets_status_deferred** - Status changes to deferred
2. **defer_indefinitely_no_until** - No defer_until when no --until
3. **defer_with_until_timestamp** - defer_until set correctly
4. **defer_multiple_issues** - All issues deferred
5. **defer_json_output** - JSON shows updated issue

### Natural Time Parsing (6 tests)
6. **defer_until_tomorrow** - \`--until tomorrow\` works
7. **defer_until_relative** - \`--until +1h\` works
8. **defer_until_specific_date** - \`--until 2026-02-01\` works
9. **defer_until_datetime** - \`--until "2026-02-01 09:00"\` works
10. **defer_until_past_warning** - Past date warns but allows
11. **defer_until_invalid_error** - Invalid time format errors

### Undefer (4 tests)
12. **undefer_sets_status_open** - Status changes to open
13. **undefer_clears_defer_until** - defer_until becomes null
14. **undefer_multiple_issues** - All issues undeferred
15. **undefer_json_output** - JSON shows updated issue

### Edge Cases (4 tests)
16. **defer_already_deferred** - Idempotent, updates defer_until
17. **undefer_already_open** - No-op, no error
18. **defer_closed_issue_error** - Cannot defer closed issue
19. **defer_nonexistent_error** - Unknown ID errors

### Ready/Blocked Interaction (3 tests)
20. **deferred_not_in_ready** - Deferred issues excluded from ready
21. **deferred_not_blocked** - Deferred != blocked
22. **undefer_appears_in_ready** - Undeferred issue shows in ready

## Logging Requirements
- Log each defer/undefer action
- Log time parsing results
- Log status transitions
- Log any validation errors

## Test File Structure
\`\`\`
tests/e2e_defer.rs
├── mod defer_basic_tests (5)
├── mod defer_time_parsing_tests (6)
├── mod undefer_tests (4)
├── mod defer_edge_cases (4)
└── mod defer_ready_interaction (3)
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file: tests/e2e_defer.rs
- [ ] 22 test functions minimum
- [ ] Natural time parsing coverage
- [ ] ready/blocked interaction verified","notes":"2026-01-18: Ran cargo test --test e2e_defer; 22/22 passing. Close blocked by beads_rust-oxmd; requested unblock.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderWaterfall","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:19:08.288701602Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T01:09:53.212345166Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T01:09:53.212345166Z","close_reason":"E2E defer/undefer tests complete (22/22 passing)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":120,"issue_id":"beads_rust-fmxd","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verified tests/e2e_defer.rs: 22 #[test] cases with logging, time parsing, ready/blocked interactions. Attempted close, but blocked by parent beads_rust-oxmd; leaving open pending parent.","created_at":"2026-01-18T00:41:49Z"},{"id":121,"issue_id":"beads_rust-fmxd","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Re-ran on 2026-01-18: cargo test --test e2e_defer -- --nocapture => 22/22 passing; previous failures not reproduced.","created_at":"2026-01-18T00:50:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-g1ig","title":"Conformance: human-readable output parity (text)","description":"Compare bd vs br text output for stable commands and normalize color/whitespace.

Scope
- Commands: list/show/ready/blocked/stats/changelog/orphans (and any others with stable text).
- Strip ANSI colors, normalize whitespace/line endings, and compare output.
- Record diffs with context in artifacts.

Acceptance
- Parity checks run in conformance mode with clear diffs on failure.
- Uses same normalization rules as golden snapshots.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Opus-4.5","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T03:55:39.591665011Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T06:06:45.158947364Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T06:06:45.158947364Z","close_reason":"Conformance tests implemented in tests/conformance_text_output.rs. Tests identify key differences: empty list messages, issue format ([P2] [task] vs [● P2] [task] -), section headers (Ready to work vs 📋 Ready work), field labels, and exit codes for not-found cases.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-g1ig","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ag35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-g1ig","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-bfgw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-g1ig","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-hdc0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-g1ig","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-lsht","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-g1ig","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-r23m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-g2w3","title":"[MEDIUM] Missing JSONL import coverage for mixed-case Status/IssueType","description":"Root cause: src/model/mod.rs:50 and src/model/mod.rs:186 now deserialize Status and IssueType case-insensitively, but existing coverage in tests/proptest_model_roundtrip.rs:64 only exercises enum serde snippets and content_hash_from_parts; tests/proptest_jsonl_roundtrip.rs exports canonical enum values, so it never imports raw JSONL containing mixed-case known values. Repro: write one JSONL issue with status="In_Progress" and issue_type="Bug", import_from_jsonl, then export_to_jsonl; the storage row and exported issue should normalize to Status::InProgress and IssueType::Bug with a content_hash identical to the canonical issue. Proposed fix: extend tests/proptest_jsonl_roundtrip.rs or add a focused storage/sync regression that hand-writes mixed-case JSONL and asserts import/export identity, normalized canonical output, and content_hash stability. Edge cases: INPROGRESS alias, DRAFT, TOMBSTONE/PINNED, and mixed-case custom variants preserved verbatim when not matching a known value.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc_2","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:22.155763266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:52:03.690287336Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":312,"issue_id":"beads_rust-g2w3","author":"cod_1","text":"degraded-coordination: files: tests/proptest_model_roundtrip.rs","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:49:45Z"},{"id":313,"issue_id":"beads_rust-g2w3","author":"cod_2","text":"degraded-coordination: files: tests/proptest_jsonl_roundtrip.rs","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:49:55Z"}]}
+{"id":"beads_rust-g2w3","title":"[MEDIUM] Missing JSONL import coverage for mixed-case Status/IssueType","description":"Root cause: src/model/mod.rs:50 and src/model/mod.rs:186 now deserialize Status and IssueType case-insensitively, but existing coverage in tests/proptest_model_roundtrip.rs:64 only exercises enum serde snippets and content_hash_from_parts; tests/proptest_jsonl_roundtrip.rs exports canonical enum values, so it never imports raw JSONL containing mixed-case known values. Repro: write one JSONL issue with status="In_Progress" and issue_type="Bug", import_from_jsonl, then export_to_jsonl; the storage row and exported issue should normalize to Status::InProgress and IssueType::Bug with a content_hash identical to the canonical issue. Proposed fix: extend tests/proptest_jsonl_roundtrip.rs or add a focused storage/sync regression that hand-writes mixed-case JSONL and asserts import/export identity, normalized canonical output, and content_hash stability. Edge cases: INPROGRESS alias, DRAFT, TOMBSTONE/PINNED, and mixed-case custom variants preserved verbatim when not matching a known value.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc_2","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:22.155763266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:54:15.553223096Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:54:15.552871357Z","close_reason":"Added sync-surface JSONL import regression covering mixed-case Status/IssueType normalization and content_hash stability.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":312,"issue_id":"beads_rust-g2w3","author":"cod_1","text":"degraded-coordination: files: tests/proptest_model_roundtrip.rs","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:49:45Z"},{"id":313,"issue_id":"beads_rust-g2w3","author":"cod_2","text":"degraded-coordination: files: tests/proptest_jsonl_roundtrip.rs","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:49:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-g3i","title":"Phase 1: Foundation - Project Setup & Core Types","description":"# Phase 1: Foundation

## Goals
Establish scaffolding, core types, schema, and storage primitives needed for all commands.

## Deliverables
- Project scaffolding (`Cargo.toml`, toolchain, layout) with YAML config deps.
- Model types (Issue/Dependency/Comment/Event) matching classic JSON shape.
- ID generation + content hashing (base36 adaptive, child counters).
- SQLite schema compatibility + migrations.
- Storage core with transaction discipline and dirty/event hooks.
- Error handling + output conventions.

## Dependencies
- Must complete before Phase 2 (core commands).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema matches bd (PRAGMA table_info/indexes).
- Unit tests for model validation + hashing.
- Storage CRUD works in memory + file DB.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:10:50.667984683Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T08:57:02.794628193Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T08:57:02.794628193Z","close_reason":"Completed Phase 1: Foundation (scaffolding, models, storage, schema, logging, tests)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-g3xk","title":"E2E tests: epic command","description":"# E2E Tests for `epic` Command

## Commands to Test
- `br epic status <epic_id>` - Show epic status with children
- `br epic add <epic_id> <issue_id>` - Add issue to epic
- `br epic remove <epic_id> <issue_id>` - Remove issue from epic
- `br epic close-eligible <epic_id>` - Check if epic can be closed

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. Create epic, add child issues, verify status
2. Epic status shows child count and completion %
3. close-eligible returns true when all children closed
4. close-eligible returns false with open children
5. Remove child from epic

### Error Cases
6. Epic status on non-epic issue → error
7. Add issue to non-existent epic → error
8. Add epic to itself (cycle) → error

### Edge Cases
9. Nested epics (epic containing epic)
10. Epic with 0 children
11. Epic with 100+ children
12. Auto-close epic when last child closed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file: tests/e2e_epic.rs
- [ ] 12+ test functions
- [ ] Tests verify parent-child dependency semantics","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CedarVale","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:26:04.584308742Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T17:18:11.572352449Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T17:18:11.572352449Z","close_reason":"Created comprehensive E2E test suite for epic command with 12 tests covering: epic status progress tracking, eligibility detection, close-eligible command, dry-run mode, eligible-only filter, childless epics, nested epics, no-epics message, partial progress, closed epics filtering, and dependency removal on delete. All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-g5dr","title":"Define MISSING_IDS_PREVIEW_LIMIT const shared between sync and CLI","description":"SUBTASK 3.3: The number 10 is used as a preview limit for displaying missing issue IDs during export safety checks. It appears in TWO modules:

Location 1 — src/sync/mod.rs:1400:
  .len().min(10) and if missing_list.len() > 10 { format!("... and {} more", ...) }

Location 2 — src/cli/commands/sync.rs:512,519:
  .len().min(10) and if missing_list.len() > 10 { ... }

This is cross-module duplication — both the sync engine and the CLI sync command independently limit how many missing IDs to show.

CHANGES:
- Add in sync/mod.rs: pub(crate) const MISSING_IDS_PREVIEW_LIMIT: usize = 10;
- Update sync/mod.rs:1400 to use the const
- Update cli/commands/sync.rs:512,519 to import and use the const from sync module

DESIGN CHOICE: Put the const in sync/mod.rs (not CLI) because the sync engine is the authoritative source for this logic. The CLI is a consumer.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:54.764362711Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T04:02:27.983304096Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T04:02:27.982837572Z","close_reason":"MISSING_IDS_PREVIEW_LIMIT pub(crate) const in sync/mod.rs, imported by cli/commands/sync.rs. Cross-module duplication eliminated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-cutting"]}
